description: Performs a 3-level Haar wavelet decomposition by iterated pairwise averaging (approximation) and differencing (detail). The score is the sum of squared detail coefficients at levels 2 and 3, which capture transients in the 16-32 Hz and 8-16 Hz bands respectively at 128 Hz sampling. Level 1 detail (32-64 Hz) is discarded as it is dominated by EMG noise. A soft-thresholding step zeros out detail coefficients below a learned noise floor.
notes: Haar wavelets are trivially cheap (just add/subtract pairs) and provide time-frequency localization. Discarding level 1 removes high-frequency EMG contamination. Soft thresholding implements wavelet denoising ala Donoho-Johnstone. Higher thresholds needed because the wavelet detail energy is much smaller scale than raw spike scores.
